help_hubs.name: Kaneʻohe District Park Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: The sole remaining open evacuation shelter on Oahu as of Saturday evening, March 21. All other city-operated shelters closed at 6 p.m. Provides cots, food, and water. Pets allowed in crates.
help_hubs.organization: City and County of Honolulu / American Red Cross
help_hubs.hours: Open as of 2026-03-21 19:00 HST; duration depends on conditions
help_hubs.notes: All other Oahu city shelters closed at 6 p.m. March 21. This is the only active city-operated shelter as of Saturday evening. Confirm status before traveling.

help_hubs.name: George Fred Wright Wahiawa District Park Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: City evacuation shelter opened March 20 for residents displaced by North Shore and Central Oahu flooding. Scheduled to close 6 p.m. March 21 per HNL Alert. Verify current status before traveling.
help_hubs.notes: Scheduled to close 6 p.m. March 21 per official HNL Alert. Previously received evacuees transferred from Waialua High School after that site closed due to dam threat. Verify before traveling. | Status was unknown/scheduled-closed as of source collection. Likely closed as of 2026-03-22. Retained in dataset — may reopen. Re-verify when government offices resume (Mon 2026-03-23 or later).

help_hubs.name: The Salvation Army Kroc Center Hawaii Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: Evacuation shelter opened by Salvation Army during the Kona low storm. Scheduled to close noon Saturday March 21 per city officials. Verify current status.
help_hubs.notes: City officials noted this shelter would close at noon Saturday March 21. Salvation Army is continuing broader disaster relief operations statewide. Call ahead to confirm. | Status was unknown/scheduled-closed as of source collection. Likely closed as of 2026-03-22. Retained in dataset — may reopen. Re-verify when government offices resume (Mon 2026-03-23 or later).

help_hubs.name: Leilehua High School Assembly Area
help_hubs.description: Assembly area opened for North Shore evacuees, including those transferred from the closed Waialua High School site. Scheduled to close 6 p.m. March 21. Supplies were being staged here during active operations.
help_hubs.notes: Photo documentation confirmed supplies being staged here March 20. Scheduled to close 6 p.m. March 21 per HNL Alert. Verify before traveling. | Status was unknown/scheduled-closed as of source collection. Likely closed as of 2026-03-22. Retained in dataset — may reopen. Re-verify when government offices resume (Mon 2026-03-23 or later).

help_hubs.name: Kahuku Elementary School - LāHui Foundation Donation Drop-Off
help_hubs.description: LāHui Foundation accepting flood recovery supplies at Kahuku Elementary School. Recovery items only — no household goods. Open 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.
help_hubs.notes: Recovery supplies only. No household items accepted. LāHui is also mobilizing cleanup crews and coordinating volunteer distribution.

help_hubs.name: North Shore Salon and Spa - Donation Collection and Sorting
help_hubs.description: Donation collection and sorting site on the North Shore operating Sunday March 22, 8 a.m. to 5 p.m.
help_hubs.notes: Listed in KHON2 community recovery roundup for March 22. Volunteers also needed for sorting.

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Foodbank - Mililani Park & Ride Food Distribution
help_hubs.description: Emergency food distribution for flood-impacted residents hosted by Hawaii Foodbank at the Mililani Park and Ride. Open Sunday March 22 starting at 9 a.m. Supplies distributed while available.
help_hubs.hours: Sun Mar 22: starting 9:00 a.m., while supplies last
help_hubs.notes: For flood-impacted residents of Central Oahu and North Shore. First-come, first-served. Foodbank CEO confirmed additional North Shore and Central Oahu distributions on March 22 at city press conference.

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Foodbank - North Shore Mobile Food Distribution
help_hubs.description: Emergency mobile food distribution routing through the North Shore with stops in Hauula, Laie, and up to Sunset Beach. For flood-impacted individuals and families. Times and specific stop locations may vary.
help_hubs.hours: Sun Mar 22; times and stops may vary - check hawaiifoodbank.org
help_hubs.notes: Stops confirmed in Hauula, Laie, and Sunset Beach area. Exact timing variable. Distribution while supplies last.

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Foodbank - Our Lady of Sorrows Church Food Distribution, Wahiawa
help_hubs.description: Emergency food distribution co-hosted by Hawaii Foodbank and Our Lady of Sorrows Church for Central Oahu flood-impacted residents. Held Saturday March 21, 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. Advance registration was encouraged.
help_hubs.organization: Hawaii Foodbank / Our Lady of Sorrows Church
help_hubs.notes: Held March 21. May recur; check hawaiifoodbank.org for updated schedule. Registration link was available at igfn.us/form/xfK07Q.

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Foodbank - Key Project Food Distribution, Kahaluu
help_hubs.description: Emergency food distribution co-hosted by Hawaii Foodbank and Key Project for North Shore and Windward Oahu flood-impacted residents. Held Saturday March 21, noon to 2 p.m.
help_hubs.notes: Held March 21. Intended for North Shore and Windward Oahu flood-impacted residents. Check hawaiifoodbank.org for updated or recurring events.

help_hubs.name: Boil Water Notice - Mokuleia to Turtle Bay (North Shore)
help_hubs.description: Board of Water Supply has issued a boil water notice for all water users from Mokuleia to Turtle Bay. Do not consume tap water without boiling first. Notice is in effect until further notice.
help_hubs.hours: In effect until further notice as of 4:15 p.m. March 21, 2026
help_hubs.notes: Issued via HNL Alert 4:15 p.m. March 21, 2026. Affects all water users between Mokuleia and Turtle Bay. Check boardofwatersupply.com/storm for updates.

help_hubs.name: City and County of Honolulu - Oahu Storm Damage Self-Report Form
help_hubs.description: Online form for Oahu residents to self-report damage to homes and property from the Kona low storm. Used by city and county to assess recovery needs.
help_hubs.notes: Online form only. Intended for residents in storm-impacted areas across Oahu to report home and property damage.

help_hubs.name: American Red Cross - Hawaii Storm Response Volunteer Signup
help_hubs.description: American Red Cross is actively recruiting volunteers to support Hawaii storm response operations, including shelter staffing and direct services to displaced residents.
help_hubs.notes: Online signup form available. Red Cross also provides shelter check-in and support; no ID required at shelters.

help_hubs.name: LāHui Foundation - Flood Cleanup Volunteer Signup
help_hubs.description: LāHui Foundation is mobilizing cleanup crews for flood-impacted North Shore areas and coordinating distribution of donated supplies. Volunteers can sign up via online form.
help_hubs.notes: Accepting cleanup volunteers and recovery supply donations. Supply drop-off at Kahuku Elementary School, 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. (recovery items only, no household goods).

help_hubs.name: Waialua Community Cleanup - Waialua Beach Road and Cane Haul Road
help_hubs.description: Community-organized flood cleanup at the intersection of Waialua Beach Road and Cane Haul Road. Sunday March 22 at 10 a.m.
help_hubs.address: Waialua Beach Rd and Cane Haul Rd, Waialua, HI 96791
help_hubs.notes: Community-organized; sourced from KHON2 community roundup for March 22. Organizer not named - confirm via KHON2 article before traveling.

help_hubs.name: Shore Cleanup - North Shore Stables, Waialua Road
help_hubs.description: Shore cleanup hosted by One Ocean Conservation and Save the Sea Turtles International at the North Shore Stables gates off Waialua Road. Sunday March 22, 1 p.m. to 3 p.m.
help_hubs.organization: One Ocean Conservation / Save the Sea Turtles International
help_hubs.address: North Shore Stables, off Waialua Rd, Oahu, HI
help_hubs.notes: Focused on shoreline debris cleanup post-flooding. Organized nonprofits named.

help_hubs.name: Aloha United Way - Flood Victim Help Line
help_hubs.description: Aloha United Way is operating a help call and text line to connect flooding victims with resources and support services.
help_hubs.notes: Call or text line for flooding victims seeking resources. Check auw.org for current helpline number.

help_hubs.name: The Salvation Army - Hawaii Kona Low Disaster Relief
help_hubs.description: The Salvation Army is conducting disaster relief efforts statewide, including financial assistance applications and material support. Working with city partners on Oahu. Information on financial assistance applications forthcoming per March 21 press conference.
help_hubs.organization: The Salvation Army, Hawaii and Pacific Islands Division
help_hubs.notes: Financial assistance application process being announced. Divisional Commander Major Troy Trimmer confirmed operations at March 21 city press conference.

help_hubs.name: American Red Cross - Hawaii Shelter Locator and Emergency Line
help_hubs.description: The Red Cross maintains real-time open shelter information for all islands. Find current open locations at redcross.org, via the Red Cross Emergency App, or by calling 1-800-RED CROSS. No ID required. Phone charging available at shelters.
help_hubs.notes: Real-time shelter list maintained at redcross.org. No ID required at any Red Cross shelter. Phone charging and emotional support available.

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Emergency Management Agency - March 2026 Kona Low Resource Hub
help_hubs.description: Official state hub for the March 2026 Kona Low event. Includes real-time HNL Alert updates, shelter information, evacuation zone maps, donation guidance, damage reporting links, and multilingual resources.
help_hubs.notes: Authoritative state source. Includes links to county damage reporting forms for Oahu and Maui, shelter maps, and recommended donation channels (Salvation Army, Red Cross, Stronger Hawaii Fund).

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Community Foundation - Stronger Hawaii Fund
help_hubs.description: Disaster relief fund operated by Hawaii Community Foundation accepting monetary donations to support communities statewide affected by the Kona low flooding.
help_hubs.notes: Recommended by HI-EMA as an official donation channel. Covers statewide needs including Oahu, Maui, and neighbor islands.

help_hubs.name: Hawaii Farmers Union United - Hawaii Flood Response Fund
help_hubs.description: Dedicated flood response fund created by Hawaii Farmers Union United to support farmers and rural communities impacted by the Kona low flooding. Monetary donations accepted via PayPal.
help_hubs.notes: Specifically targeted at farmers and rural areas hard-hit by flooding.

help_hubs.name: South Maui Community Park Gym Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: Active evacuation shelter in South Maui providing cots, food, and water for residents displaced by Kona low flooding. Still open as of March 21 afternoon.
help_hubs.organization: MEMA / Maui County Parks and Recreation / American Red Cross
help_hubs.hours: Open as of 2026-03-21 afternoon; duration depends on conditions
help_hubs.notes: Pet-friendly with crates. Maui Humane Society supplied extra crates at this location. Two other Maui shelters (Baldwin High and Kihei Community Center) closed at noon March 21.

help_hubs.name: Hana High School Cafeteria Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: Evacuation shelter in East Maui providing food and water for residents displaced or unable to travel due to Hana Highway closures and flood conditions.
help_hubs.organization: MEMA / Maui County Parks and Recreation / American Red Cross
help_hubs.hours: Open as of 2026-03-21 afternoon; duration depends on conditions
help_hubs.notes: Hana Highway restricted to emergency vehicles, essential workers, and local traffic. Access limited.

help_hubs.name: Lahaina Civic Center Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: Evacuation shelter in West Maui for residents displaced by flooding. Note: access is via the Napili side off Honoapiilani Highway due to road closures.
help_hubs.organization: MEMA / Maui County Parks and Recreation / American Red Cross
help_hubs.hours: Open as of 2026-03-21 afternoon; duration depends on conditions
help_hubs.notes: Access via Napili side off highway only due to Honoapiilani Hwy closures. A large sinkhole closed Lilioa St and N. Laalo St in Lahaina as of Saturday evening.
help_hubs.accessibility_notes: Pet-friendly if animals are crated; access route restricted

help_hubs.name: Baldwin High School Gym Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: Central Maui shelter reopened Saturday evening March 21 at 7 p.m. due to new road closures and evacuation warnings from Kona storm 2. Provides cots, food, and water.
help_hubs.organization: MEMA / Maui County Parks and Recreation / American Red Cross
help_hubs.hours: Reopened 7:00 p.m. 2026-03-21; duration depends on conditions
help_hubs.notes: Previously closed at noon March 21, then reopened at 7 p.m. March 21 due to second Kona storm impacts and new road closures.

help_hubs.name: Kaunakakai Gym Evacuation Shelter
help_hubs.description: Evacuation shelter opened March 22 at 7 a.m. due to severe flash flooding from Kona storm 2 on Molokai. Provides cots, food, and water. Elevated water levels reported in Kawela Gulch and Halawa Stream.
help_hubs.organization: MEMA / Maui County Parks and Recreation / American Red Cross
help_hubs.hours: Opened 7:00 a.m. 2026-03-22; duration depends on conditions
help_hubs.notes: Opened same-day in response to flash flooding on Molokai. Flash flood warning was in effect until approximately 8:45 a.m. March 22. Flood watch for Maui County through Sunday.

help_hubs.name: Maui Food Bank - Emergency Food Distribution
help_hubs.description: Maui Food Bank is delivering food to Hana and other flood-impacted areas of Maui County.
help_hubs.notes: Confirmed active deliveries to Hana and other hard-hit areas. Specific distribution site schedule not published at time of collection.

help_hubs.name: Maui Rapid Response - Emergency Supply Distribution
help_hubs.description: Maui Rapid Response is distributing tarps, flashlights, first-aid kits, bottled water, and other emergency supplies to homeless communities and other vulnerable populations on Maui.
help_hubs.notes: Focused on homeless communities and vulnerable populations. Distributing tarps, first-aid kits, flashlights, and water. Distribution locations not specified in source.

help_hubs.name: Maui United Way - Flood Relief Donations
help_hubs.description: Maui United Way is collecting monetary donations to support Maui residents affected by recent flooding and other disasters.
help_hubs.notes: Monetary donations only. For broader disaster-related needs on Maui.

help_hubs.name: Hawaiian Council - Kakoo Oahu Donation Campaign
help_hubs.description: The Hawaiian Council launched the Kakoo Oahu campaign on March 21 to support individuals and families impacted by severe weather on Oahu. Funds will address housing stability, direct financial assistance, and other critical support. The council will match every donation dollar-for-dollar up to $100,000.
help_hubs.notes: Dollar-for-dollar matching up to $100,000. Addresses housing stability and direct financial assistance for impacted families. Donation URL not captured; check hawaiinewsnow.com article for link. | Donation URL confirmed: https://givebutter.com/support-the-north-shore-community-4jobpv

public_need_summaries.title: Flood Cleanup Volunteers Needed - North Shore Oahu
public_need_summaries.description: Multiple organizations including LāHui Foundation, One Ocean Conservation, and community groups are actively requesting cleanup volunteers for Waialua, Haleiwa, and surrounding North Shore areas as of March 22. Organized events are running today.

public_need_summaries.title: Boil Water Advisory in Effect - North Shore Oahu (Mokuleia to Turtle Bay)
public_need_summaries.description: Board of Water Supply issued a boil water notice effective 4:15 p.m. March 21 for all residents between Mokuleia and Turtle Bay. Bottled water and water distribution for affected residents may be needed. Notice is in effect until further notice.

public_need_summaries.title: Emergency Food Distribution Needed - North Shore and Central Oahu
public_need_summaries.description: Hawaii Foodbank CEO publicly confirmed at March 21 city press conference that emergency food distributions are needed and are being organized for North Shore and Central Oahu flood-impacted residents. Distributions running March 22.

public_need_summaries.title: Financial Assistance Applications Opening for Displaced Residents - Statewide
public_need_summaries.description: The Salvation Army confirmed at the March 21 city press conference that information on financial assistance applications for flood-impacted residents would be released. Applications not yet publicly open as of March 22 morning.
